mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals labor market recovery, boosting consumer spending and equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 8.0 percent, contradicting the predicted positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous low federal funds rates and stable consumer prices compress real discount rates while preserving purchasing power, generating positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive SPX 60-day returns do not occur when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 percent and CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth remains under 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates supply-side capacity expansion, reducing input cost pressures and supporting corporate margins, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth correlates negatively with 5-day SPX returns, or industrial output increases fail to improve forward equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts reflect strong construction demand and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 50 units month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are flat or negative when HOUST month-over-month growth exceeds 50 units, invalidating the demand signal.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust employment growth, supporting aggregate demand and equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02 across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with moderating consumer prices indicate real income growth and healthy demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when RSAFS increases and CPIAUCSL growth moderates, demonstrating the interaction signal fails.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive despite FEDFUNDS increases during tightening periods, indicating rate hikes do not suppress short-term equity performance.
